medical_history.chief_complaint: acute onset of a rapidly enlarging, ulcerative nodule on her left lower eyelid
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: The lesion began 3 weeks prior to presentation and was attributed to a presumed spider bite. The patient had discovered a spider in her bed around the time the lesion began. Previous therapies included oral antibiotics, oral corticosteroids, as well as antibiotic and corticosteroid eye drops. An incision and drainage procedure was attempted but no fluid or pus was expressed.
medical_history.past_medical_history: ulcerative colitis, diagnosed at 8 years of age and controlled with a combination of oral 6-mercaptopurine (6-MP), 75 mg daily, and oral mesalamine (delayed release mesalamine monosodium salt), 1200 mg three times daily. She denied symptoms of active ulcerative colitis. Five weeks prior to presentation, she was diagnosed with IM by a positive heterophile antibody in the setting of fever, sore throat, headache, and fatigue.

physical_examination.general_examination: a 3 cm indurated, tender, erythematous, violaceous ulcerated, crusted nodule on the left lower eyelid. Mild subconjunctival hemorrhage was observed. Lymphadenopathy was not present in the cervical chain and no other relevant cutaneous findings were observed.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Low white blood cell count of 3.72 K/μL (reference range 4.5–13.0 K/μL) with normal monocyte percentage on the differential but slightly increased percentage of neutrophils (70%; upper limit of normal is 67%), and a low red blood cell count at 3.78 m/μL (reference range 4.0–5.2 m/μL).
Negative results. An atypical p-ANCA (1:80 titer) was present.

Nodular, mixed lymphocytic dermal infiltrate extending through the skeletal muscle at the base of the biopsy. Medium to large round atypical lymphoid cells focally containing prominent nucleoli were noted and some cells demonstrated a plasmacytoid appearance. The medium to large lymphocytes stained positively for CD20 and for EBV using the EBV-encoded RNA (EBER) immunolabel, while small admixed lymphocytes stained positively with CD3. The subset of lymphoplasmacytoid cells labeled with CD79a. Pertinent negatives included negative CD10, TdT, CD56, CD30, CD138, and HHV8. Bcl-2 stained intermixed background T lymphocytes. Ki-67 labeled about 80% of cells.

radiographic_examinations.findings: Negative except for the cutaneous site.

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on the patient's history of immunosuppression due to ulcerative colitis treatment with 6-mercaptopurine and mesalamine, recent infectious mononucleosis, and the biopsy findings of an Epstein-Barr virus-positive lymphoproliferative disorder. The lesion resolved completely after discontinuation of 6-mercaptopurine and treatment with cyclophosphamide, supporting the diagnosis.
